body_txt: A Framing Device is set up in which the hero recounts his adventures in vivid detail. Very vivid detail. So vivid, in fact, that once the story is over and Fridge Logic has set in the question arises: how are they remembering all these details, while fighting off Ninja Pirate Zombie Robot s in Space ? This trope is especially common in the Epistolary Novel , where action scenes and lengthy discussions are minutely reproduced afterwards in the form of journal entries or letters, which is assumed to be exactly what happened/was said. Obviously more common in literature due to the need to describe everything, but a variant can appear in television when details that would be insignificant or assumed knowledge are commented on by characters from the framing device, implying that the speaker really was describing everything the viewer sees on screen. Essentially the opposite of Unreliable Narrator . See also Direct Line to the Author , Exposition Intuition , Literary Agent Hypothesis , All First-Person Narrators Write Like Novelists .
